trails.name: Flagstaff-pineapple walkway
trails.description: The Flagstaff – Pineapple Walkway can be accessed from Flagstaff-Whare Flat Road at the west end or from Booth Road at the east.

trails.name: Millenium walkway, moeraki
trails.description: Section From Centery Park To Moeraki Esplanade Reserve

trails.name: Flagstaff-pineapple walkway
trails.description: The Flagstaff – Pineapple Walkway can be accessed from Flagstaff-Whare Flat Road at the west end or from Booth Road at the east.

trails.name: Swampy ridge track
trails.description: Swampy Ridge Track can be accessed from the carpark on Semple Road in the settlement of Waitati, or from the Pineapple – Flagstaff Walkway.

trails.name: Trotters Gorge Track
trails.description: A tramping track with sea views and some large podocarp trees. This is the longest track at Trotters Gorge.

trails.name: Te Makarara / Outram - Lee Stream Track
Glencreag Track
trails.description: A steady climb to Big Hut, 1350 m above sea level.

trails.name: Last Creek Track
trails.description: An access track leading to the Rock and Pillar Conservation Area.

trails.name: Rockvale Track
trails.description: The northernmost access track to the Rock and Pillar Conservation Area.

trails.name: Brookdale Track
trails.description: A short access track from Rock Road to the Rock and Pillar Conservation Area.

trails.name: March Creek Track
trails.description: An access track leading from McKinnon Road to the Rock and Pillar Conservation Area.

trails.name: Government Track
trails.description: Walk or mountain bike the Government Track which traverses the slopes of the beech-and mānuka-forested Waipori Valley.

trails.name: Careys Creek Track
trails.description: This track offers a challenging walk. There are plenty of opportunities for birdwatching and the chance to discover an area of Ōtepoti/Dunedin.

trails.name: Highcliff Track
trails.description: This track provides stunning views of the Otago coastline along the length of the Otago Peninsula.

trails.name: Trotters Cave Walk
trails.description: A walking track with native forest and caves.

trails.name: Woodside Glen Walk
trails.description: A short walk through native forest full of culture and spiritual significance.

trails.name: Grahams Bush Walkway
trails.description: Climb through native bush with views of Otago Harbour and Otago Peninsula.

trails.name: Chalkies Track
trails.description: See across Silverpeaks and Taieri Plains. Cross Silver Stream and Leishmans Creek and make the steep 400 m climb to the top of Powder Hill for a challenging tramp with spectacular views.

trails.name: Stonehurst Track
trails.description: Go up and over the Rock and Pillar Range.

trails.name: Rock and Pillar Range North Track
Trotters Creek Track
trails.description: A gently graded tramping track with native forest and views of the bluffs.

trails.name: Buskin, Highcliff and Paradise Road tracks
trails.description: Walk from Highcliff Road.

trails.name: Grahams Bush Track
trails.description: Climb through native bush with views of Otago Harbour and Otago Peninsula.

trails.name: Hawksbury lagoon and beach walk
trails.description: Walk around Hawksbury lagoon in Waikouaiti and enjoy this wildlife refuge providing habitat for ducks and native birds which can be seen from the causeways that cross the pools.

trails.name: Heyward Point Track
trails.description: This cliff top track, through farm land and regenerating forest, offers great coastal views.

trails.name: Huriawa Pā Walk
trails.description: Walk around Huriawa Peninsula on a well-formed track suitable for children - take in spectacular views and read plaques with the history of the area.

trails.name: Leith Saddle Track
trails.description: This tramping track travels through the cloud forest above the Leith Saddle on Dunedin's Northern Motorway.

trails.name: Long Beach Walk
trails.description: Visit Long Beach on this family and dog friendly walk and have a picnic at the reserve.

trails.name: Orokonui lagoon walk
trails.description: Walk around the Orokonui lagoon through native bush and view the natural wetland providing habitat for native fish and foraging birds.

trails.name: Outram Glen to Lee Stream Walk
trails.description: A very pleasant forested walking track that follows the Taieri River round to the confluence of Lee Stream with the river.

trails.name: Sandfly Bay Track
trails.description: Walk to Sandfly Bay and enjoy beautiful coastal views.

trails.name: Sandymount Track
trails.description: Walk to Sandymount and enjoy spectacular views of the coastline.

trails.name: Silver Peaks routes
trails.description: The Silver Peaks Scenic Reserve provides challenging routes in rugged country, less than 30 minutes drive from Dunedin city.

trails.name: Sutton Salt Lake walk
trails.description: Take a self-guided loop walk around Sutton Salt Lake, New Zealand's only inland salt lake.

trails.name: Trotters Gorge tracks
trails.description: Discover river sculpted caves or great views out to sea, as well as see and hear many of New Zealand's bush birds, on these tracks from Trotters Gorge Campsite.

trails.name: Tunnel Beach Track
trails.description: This short walk goes through an excavated tunnel kids will love, to a secluded beach and a spectacular, rocky coastline.

trails.name: Maukaatua Track
trails.description: A place of significance to Ngāi Tahu, Maukaatua gazes over the Taieri plains to the coast and inland over rolling tussock-covered hills.

trails.name: Aramoana Heyward Point Track
trails.description: Can be accessed off Aramoana Road, 50 m before Pari Street OR the end of Heyward Point Road, which is off the road From Port Chalmers to Purakanui and Long Beach

trails.name: Flagstaff Pineapple Walkway
trails.description: The Flagstaff – Pineapple Walkway can be accessed from Flagstaff-Whare Flat Road at the west end or from Booth Road at the east.

trails.name: Grahams Bush Walkway
trails.description: Climb through native bush with views of Otago Harbour and Otago Peninsula.

trails.name: Leith Saddle Track
trails.description: This tramping track travels through the cloud forest above the Leith Saddle on Dunedin's Northern Motorway.

trails.name: Mount Cargill Walk
trails.description: Mount Cargill Walk can be accessed from the Mount Cargill Summit at the end of Cowan Road or from Bethunes Gully off Norwood Street.
